dimension_results.detail: Root cause claim is directly verified: line 129 of hooks/userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh reads `} 9> "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null` exactly as BA states. The Read tool evidence (offset 95, line 129) confirms the bug site. The Bash left-to-right redirect ordering principle is a deterministic specification behaviour documented in the same file at lines 116-117. No BA claim requires runtime reproduction to be credible — the ordering bug is mechanically certain from source inspection. diagnosis_completeness: 'inferred only' is an accurate self-assessment by BA and does not undermine the finding, since the principle is deterministic.

The user requirement is: 'Fix the LOCK_FILE EISDIR stderr leak in hooks/userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh at line 129. Change `} 9> "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null` to `} 2>/dev/null 9> "$LOCK_FILE"`. Add AC7c to cover this path.' BA's Must Have items cover both the redirect reorder (R1/AC_SRC) and AC7c (R2). Won't Have correctly excludes flock logic, COUNTER_FILE handling, and bundling with other spec items. No universalist claim is narrowed without authorization — the user requirement itself is already precise and the BA scope matches it exactly.

The requirement does not call for an open-ended investigation — the bug site is named explicitly by the user (line 129) and the fix is specified verbatim. BA verified the bug site via Read tool and confirmed uniqueness via grep scope search (one occurrence). The comment at lines 116-117 cross-validates the correct principle. No investigation deliverable was omitted.

hooks/userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh exists and line 129 contains the claimed text `} 9> "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null`. The LOCK_FILE variable is defined at line 64 as `LOCK_FILE="${COUNTER_FILE}.lock"`. The component_chain entry cites lines 99-130 as the brace-group and correctly identifies line 129 as the closing redirect line. All file path and line number claims are accurate.

AC_SRC grep pattern `} 2>/dev/null 9> "$LOCK_FILE"` is literal and directly matches the prescribed fix text. No substitution or paraphrase is present. The harness_recipe for AC7c uses bash to invoke the actual hook file against a real EISDIR condition — the recipe is a faithful executable encoding of the AC prose. No drift detected between AC text and the literal execution recipe.

v1_root_cause_accuracy.evidence: Read tool (offset 95, limit 41): line 129 reads exactly `  } 9> "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null`

v2_fix_correctness.rationale: Bash processes redirections left-to-right. Placing `2>/dev/null` first redirects fd-2 to /dev/null before the `9> "$LOCK_FILE"` open(2) syscall is attempted. When LOCK_FILE is a directory, the 9> open fails with EISDIR, but Bash's error message goes to fd-2 which is already /dev/null. The fix is mechanically sound. Cross-validated by lines 116-117 in the same file which document this exact ordering principle, and by lines 118 and 123 which correctly apply `2>/dev/null` before `>` for the inner COUNTER_FILE writes.

v3_ac_src_completeness.rationale: The grep pattern targets the fixed form verbatim. Combined with `bash -n` syntax check this covers both structural correctness and syntactic validity. The pattern is sufficiently specific — it is unlikely to match any unrelated line in the file.

lock_file_derivation.sanitization_rule: tr -cd 'A-Za-z0-9_-' | cut -c1-64 — all chars in 'test-enospc-ac7c' are in the allowed set; length 18 < 64

v4_ac7c_harness.directory_precreation: mkdir -p "$LOCK_FILE" pre-creates the lock path as a directory, correctly triggering the EISDIR condition when fd 9 attempts open(2) on it
v4_ac7c_harness.df_shim_format: Shim outputs 'Use%\n76%\n77%'. After `tail -n +2 | tr -d '% '`, yields '76' and '77'. Both exceed THRESHOLD=75, activating the pressure branch. Correct.
v4_ac7c_harness.rc_stdout_stderr_assertions: Harness captures stdout into $out, stderr into /tmp/ac7c_stderr, exit_code=$?. Assertions rc=0, stdout='', stderr='' are structurally correct.
v4_ac7c_harness.minor_observation: /tmp/ac7c_stderr is not included in the EXIT INT TERM trap (cleanup only removes $LOCK_FILE, ${LOCK_FILE%%.lock}, $tmpdir). The recipe ends with explicit `rm -f /tmp/ac7c_stderr` but this leaks on abnormal termination. Not a blocking correctness defect — the AC assertion logic is sound and the temp file is in /tmp (ephemeral). No finding raised.

v5_non_regression.rationale: AC7 exercises the COUNTER_FILE EISDIR path in the increment branch (line 123 guard). AC7b exercises the COUNTER_FILE EISDIR path in the saturated/mtime-refresh branch (line 118 guard). Neither line 118 nor line 123 is modified by this change. The change is confined to line 129 (the outer brace-group redirect). AC7 and AC7b will continue to pass with no modification required.

v6_scope_boundary.rationale: Grep search confirmed `} 9> "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null` appears exactly once in hooks/ (hooks/userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh:129). The Must Have items are: (1) reorder line 129, (2) add AC7c to acceptance-criteria file. Won't Have correctly excludes flock logic, rate-limit logic, COUNTER_FILE handling, other hooks, and arch-7 phase 2. Scope is tight and matches user requirement exactly.
